stimulus: Researchers Hana Okada and Daniel Brooks studied the relationship between the availability of safe sidewalks in a town and people's willingness to walk to local destinations. Their results suggest that the absence of well-maintained sidewalks can deter new or inexperienced walkers from making walking trips but has little effect on experienced walkers. Therefore, adding more well-maintained sidewalks in a town could potentially ______
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: increase the number of new or inexperienced walkers making trips in the town.
decrease the number of experienced walkers making trips in the town.
increase the number of experienced walkers making trips in the town.
decrease the number of new or inexperienced walkers making trips in the town.

rationale:
Choice A is correct. The deterrent is removed when sidewalks improve, so new/inexperienced walkers — who were the ones deterred — would be expected to increase.

Choice B reverses the relationship. Choice C contradicts the finding that lighting/sidewalk quality doesn't affect experienced walkers. Choice D contradicts the deterrence-removal logic.
